revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神勅]]
'''Divine Decrees''' (神勅, Shinchoku) refer to the commands given by deities, as well as the documents representing such commands. == Divine Decrees of Eternal Heaven and Earth == In the section on {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|qid=Q1084733|lt=Tenson kōrin|1=Tenson kōrin}} in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, the following three divine decrees (known as the Three Great Divine Decrees) were issued by {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} to her grandson {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} and others: * '''Divine Decree of Eternal Heaven and Earth''' - "The land of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni, the Land of Abundant Reed Plains and the Land of Fresh Rice Ears, shall be the realm where my descendants will reign. You, my imperial grandson, descend and govern it. Go forth! The prosperity of your imperial line shall be as eternal as heaven and earth." * '''Divine Decree of the Sacred Mirror''' - "You, my child, shall regard this sacred mirror as you regard me. Keep it with you, under the same roof, in the same palace, as a sacred object." * '''Divine Decree of the Rice Ears of the Sacred Garden''' - "Let my child use the rice ears of the Sacred Garden of the High Plain of Heaven that I have grown." Additionally, two other decrees were given in the same section—one issued by Amaterasu Ōmikami to her retainers {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}, and another by {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}—collectively known as the Five Great Divine Decrees: * '''Divine Decree of Guarding the Palace''' - "I request that you two deities also reside in the palace and diligently protect it." * '''Divine Decree of the Sacred Grove and Sacred Stone''' - "I shall establish a sacred grove and sacred stone for my imperial grandson. You, Ame-no-Koyane and Futodama, shall bring the sacred grove to Ashihara no Nakatsukuni and serve my imperial grandson." In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, similar passages appear in the sections on the subjugation of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni and Tenson kōrin. While the wording differs, both sources emphasize that it was the divine will for the descendants of Amaterasu Ōmikami to govern Japan. Since the enthronement of Ninigi's great-grandson {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, this divine lineage has been maintained by the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}}. During the prewar period, this belief served as the legal, historical, and religious foundation for the Emperor's role as Japan's sovereign (see {{ill|Bansei Ikkei|ja|万世一系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bansei Ikkei|1=Bansei ikkei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11239731}}). == Divine Decrees of the Three Munakata Goddesses == According to the Nihon Shoki, the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, who were born during the {{ill|Amaterasu and Susanoo's Pledge|ja|アマテラスとスサノオの誓約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu and Susanoo's Pledge|1=Five Male and Three Female Deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11284880}}, were enshrined on islands connecting Japan and the continent (Okitsu-miya, Nakatsu-miya, and Hetsu-miya) before the descent of the heavenly grandchild. The Nihon Shoki records Amaterasu Ōmikami's decree: "You three deities shall dwell in the path between the lands and assist my heavenly grandchild. Be worshipped for the sake of the heavenly grandchild." The three Munakata shrines represent the oldest example of clearly described divine names and enshrinement locations in the Nihon Shoki. == Commentary == Until the {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|qid=Q5308718|lt=Early Modern Period of Japan|1=Early modern period}}, the concept of divine decrees was not widely recognized outside the field of {{ill|Kokugaku|ja|Kokugakuist|1=Kokugaku|2=ja|3=国学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国学|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q855159}}. However, during the late Meiji period, with rapid modernization and the rise of forces advocating for {{ill|Republicanism|ja|共和政体|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Republicanism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共和制|1=Republic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7270}} and {{ill|Communism|en|Communism|qid=Q6186|lt=Communism|1=Communism}}, divine decrees were increasingly invoked as a theoretical foundation to counter such ideologies. During the wartime period, they became a key element supporting government-sanctioned ideologies such as {{ill|Kōkoku Shikan|ja|皇国史観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkoku Shikan|1=Kōkoku shikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5368837}} and {{ill|Kokutai|en|Kokutai|qid=Q1204868|lt=Kokutai|1=Kokutai}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shinkoku|ja|神国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkoku|1=Shinkoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5367717}} * {{ill|Revelation|en|Revelation|qid=Q192906|lt=Revelation|1=Revelation}} * {{ill|Mandate of Heaven|en|Mandate of Heaven|qid=Q875377|lt=Mandate of Heaven|1=Mandate of Heaven}} * {{ill|Divine right of kings|en|Divine right of kings|qid=Q839452|lt=Divine Right of Kings|1=Divine right of kings}} {{Shinto Horizontal|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|神勅|version=98506170|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:神勅]]
